name: Free-Space Path Loss Computation
description: Tests standalone path loss functions (fspl, rainpl, gaspl) without requiring geographic sites
prompt: Generate MATLAB code to compute the free-space path loss using fspl at 2.4 GHz over 1 km. Also compute rain attenuation using rainpl at 28 GHz over the same distance with 25 mm/hr rain rate, and gas attenuation using gaspl at 28 GHz.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses fspl function for free-space path loss
assertions.expected: Code calls fspl() to compute free-space path loss. PASS if fspl is used with distance and wavelength arguments. FAIL if path loss is computed manually or with a different function.

assertions.description: Uses rainpl for rain attenuation
assertions.expected: Code calls rainpl() for rain attenuation. PASS if rainpl is used with range, frequency, and rain rate. FAIL if rain attenuation is computed manually or with a different function.

assertions.description: Uses gaspl for gas attenuation
assertions.expected: Code calls gaspl() for atmospheric gas absorption. PASS if gaspl is used with range, frequency, and atmospheric parameters. FAIL if gas attenuation is computed manually or with a different function.

assertions.description: Uses physconst for speed of light
assertions.expected: Code uses physconst('LightSpeed') or physconst("LightSpeed") rather than a hardcoded value like 3e8. PASS if physconst is used. FAIL if speed of light is hardcoded.

assertions.description: Does not call show() in headless environment
assertions.expected: show(

name: Signal Strength Between Sites
description: Tests creation of txsite/rxsite and computation of signal strength with a propagation model
prompt: Generate MATLAB code to create a txsite at coordinates (42.36, -71.06) at 900 MHz with 20W power and 30m height. Create an rxsite 500m away. Compute the sigstrength using the free-space propagation model.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates txsite with frequency and power
assertions.expected: Code creates a txsite object with TransmitterFrequency set to 900 MHz (900e6) and TransmitterPower set to 20. PASS if txsite is created with both properties. FAIL if no transmitter site is created or frequency/power are missing.

assertions.description: Creates rxsite object
assertions.expected: Code creates an rxsite object for the receiver. PASS if rxsite is used. FAIL if no receiver site is created or a different approach is used.

assertions.description: Uses sigstrength to compute received signal level
assertions.expected: Code calls sigstrength() to compute the signal strength at the receiver. PASS if sigstrength is called with receiver, transmitter, and propagation model arguments. FAIL if signal strength is computed manually or with a different function.

assertions.description: Uses fprintf for formatted numerical output
assertions.expected: Code uses fprintf to display results with formatted numbers and units. PASS if fprintf is used for output. FAIL if results are displayed only via disp() or default variable echo.

assertions.description: Does not add title to site viewer
assertions.expected: title(

name: Composite Propagation Model for mmWave
description: Tests combining multiple propagation models with the + operator for mmWave atmospheric analysis
prompt: Generate MATLAB code to analyze signal propagation at 28 GHz between two sites 200m apart. Use propagationModel to create a composite model combining free-space, rain, and gas attenuation. Compute the sigstrength and link margin.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates composite model using + operator
assertions.expected: Code creates a composite propagation model by combining multiple propagationModel objects with the + operator (e.g., propagationModel('freespace') + propagationModel('rain') + propagationModel('gas')). PASS if + operator combines at least two models. FAIL if models are applied separately without composition.

assertions.description: Combines free-space with atmospheric models
assertions.expected: Composite model includes freespace plus at least one atmospheric model (rain or gas). PASS if freespace is combined with rain and/or gas. FAIL if only freespace is used or atmospheric effects are ignored.

assertions.description: Computes both signal strength and link margin
assertions.expected: Code computes signal strength using sigstrength() AND link margin (difference between received power and receiver sensitivity or noise floor). PASS if both metrics are computed and reported. FAIL if only one is computed.

assertions.description: Does not call show() in headless environment
assertions.expected: show(

assertions.description: Does not add title to site viewer
assertions.expected: title(

name: SINR with Multiple Transmitters
description: Tests SINR computation with multiple interfering transmitters
prompt: Generate MATLAB code to set up 3 txsite objects at 1900 MHz in Boston. Compute the sinr at a receiver location using the free-space model. Identify the strongest signal source.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates 3 transmitter sites
assertions.expected: Code creates exactly 3 txsite objects at different locations in Boston at 1900 MHz. PASS if 3 distinct txsite objects are created with TransmitterFrequency=1900e6 or 1.9e9. FAIL if fewer than 3 sites or wrong frequency.

assertions.description: Uses sinr function for interference analysis
assertions.expected: Code calls the sinr() function to compute signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio. PASS if sinr() is called with an array of transmitters. FAIL if SINR is computed manually or a different function is used.

assertions.description: Passes array of transmitters to sinr
assertions.expected: Code constructs an array of txsite objects (e.g., [tx1 tx2 tx3] or txs array) and passes it to the sinr function. PASS if transmitters are grouped into an array for sinr(). FAIL if sinr is called separately for each transmitter.

assertions.description: Identifies strongest signal source
assertions.expected: Code determines which transmitter provides the strongest signal or highest SINR at the receiver and reports it. PASS if the best-server is identified. FAIL if all transmitters are treated equally without comparison.

assertions.description: Does not call show() in headless environment
assertions.expected: show(

name: Ray Tracing for Urban mmWave
description: Tests ray tracing workflow with SBR method, reflections, and correct pathloss handling
prompt: Generate MATLAB code to perform ray tracing at 28 GHz between a transmitter at 10m height and a receiver at 1.5m height in an urban environment. Use the SBR method with 3 reflections and 1 diffraction. Compute the signal strength using the ray tracing propagation model.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses raytracing propagation model with SBR method
assertions.expected: Code creates a propagationModel('raytracing') and sets Method to 'sbr'. PASS if raytracing model is created with sbr method. FAIL if a different propagation model is used or method is not set to sbr.

assertions.description: Sets MaxNumReflections to 3
assertions.expected: Code sets MaxNumReflections=3 on the ray tracing propagation model. PASS if MaxNumReflections is set to 3. FAIL if reflections are not configured or set to a different value.

assertions.description: Sets MaxNumDiffractions to 1
assertions.expected: Code sets MaxNumDiffractions=1 on the ray tracing propagation model. PASS if MaxNumDiffractions is set to 1. FAIL if diffractions are not configured or set to a different value.

assertions.description: Computes signal strength with ray tracing model
assertions.expected: Code calls sigstrength() or raytrace() with the ray tracing propagation model to analyze the channel. PASS if signal analysis uses the configured ray tracing model. FAIL if signal strength is computed without the ray tracing model.

assertions.description: Does not call show() in headless environment
assertions.expected: show(
